Reasoning
Q1
Directions:Answer the questions based on the
information given below.
Seven persons A, B, C, D, E, F and G have
meetings on different days of a week from
Monday to Sunday but not necessarily in same
order. Each of them belongs to different
countries among Egypt, Italy, India, France,
Mexico, Germany and Spain.
D belongs to Spain. F, who belongs to Italy, does
not have meeting on Monday. Two persons have
meeting before the one, who belongs to Egypt. E
has meeting on Saturday. Person, who belongs to
Mexico, has meeting on Sunday. B has meeting
two days before A who belongs to neither Egypt
nor Mexico. Person, who belongs to Germany,
has meeting just before C, who does not belong
to Mexico.
F has meeting on ______.
(A) Tuesday
(B) Saturday
(C) Friday
(D) Thursday
(E) None of these
Q2
Answer the questions based on the information
given below.
Seven persons A, B, C, D, E, F and G have
meetings on different days of a week from
Monday to Sunday but not necessarily in same
order. Each of them belongs to different
countries among Egypt, Italy, India, France,
Mexico, Germany and Spain.
D belongs to Spain. F, who belongs to Italy, does
not have meeting on Monday. Two persons have
meeting before the one, who belongs to Egypt. E
has meeting on Saturday. Person, who belongs to
Mexico, has meeting on Sunday. B has meeting
two days before A, who belongs to neither Egypt
nor Mexico. Person, who belongs to Germany,
has meeting just before C, who does not belong
to Mexico.
How many persons have meeting before A?
(A) Four
(B) Three
(C) Two
(D) Five
(E) None of these
Q3
Answer the questions based on the information
given below.
Seven persons A, B, C, D, E, F and G have
meetings on different days of a week from
Monday to Sunday but not necessarily in same

countries among Egypt, Italy, India, France,

nor Mexico. Person, who belongs to Germany,
has meeting just before C, who does not belong
to Mexico.
Which of the following pair is correct?
(A) D, Tuesday
(B) B, Germany
(C) Monday, F
(D) Egypt, Saturday
(E) None of these
Q4
 
Scheduling Puzzles -01 
Level-1

Answer the questions based on the information
given below.
 
Seven persons A, B, C, D, E, F and G have
meetings on different days of a week from
Monday to Sunday but not necessarily in same
order. Each of them belongs to different
countries among Egypt, Italy, India, France,
Mexico, Germany and Spain.
D belongs to Spain. F, who belongs to Italy, does
not have meeting on Monday. Two persons have
meeting before the one, who belongs to Egypt. E
has meeting on Saturday. Person, who belongs to
Mexico, has meeting on Sunday. B has meeting
two days before A, who belongs to neither Egypt
nor Mexico. Person, who belongs to Germany,
has meeting just before C, who does not belong
to Mexico.
 
Who among the following belongs to France?
(A) D
(B) E
(C) B
(D) A
(E) Either A or E

Q6
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions given below.
 
Seven persons i.e L,M,N,O,P,Q and R conduct
workshop in seven different companies A, B, C, D,
E, F and G on different days of the same week
starting from Monday and ending on Sunday. All
information is not necessarily in the same order.
R conducts a workshop after Friday in company
D. Two persons conduct workshop between R
and O. L conducts workshop before O but not on
Monday. 
Two 
persons 
conduct 
workshop
between L and the person who conducts
workshop in company F. P conducts workshop in
company F but not on Saturday. P conducts
workshop either just after or just before R. Q
conducts workshop in company C but before
Thursday. N conducts workshop before M. O
conducts workshop in company A. L does not
conduct workshop in company G and B.M does
not conduct workshop in company G.
 
Who among the following person conduct
workshop on Friday?
(A) P
(B) R
(C) M
(D) O
(E) None of these
Q7
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions given below.
 
Seven persons i.e L,M,N,O,P,Q and R conduct
workshop in seven different companies A, B, C, D,
E, F and G on different days of the same week
starting from Monday and ending on Sunday. All
information is not necessarily in the same order.
R conducts workshop after Friday in company D.
Two persons conduct workshop between R and

O. L conducts workshop before O but not on
Monday. 
Two 
persons 
conduct 
workshop
between L and the person who conducts
workshop in company F. P conducts workshop in
company F but not on Saturday. P conducts
workshop either just after or just before R. Q
conducts workshop in company C but before
Thursday. N conducts workshop before M. O
conducts workshop in company A. L does not
conduct workshop in company G and B.M does
not conduct workshop in company G.
 
Four of the following five are alike in a certain
way and hence form a group. Find the one which
does not belong to that group?
(A) Q,E
(B) L,A
(C) O,G
(D) N,F
(E) R,A
Q8
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions given below.
 
Seven persons i.e L,M,N,O,P,Q and R conduct
workshop in seven different companies A, B, C, D,
E, F and G on different days of the same week
starting from Monday and ending on Sunday. All
information is not necessarily in the same order.
R conducts workshop after Friday in company D.
Two persons conduct workshop between R and
O. L conducts workshop before O but not on
Monday. 
Two 
persons 
conduct 
workshop
between L and the person who conducts
workshop in company F. P conducts workshop in
company F but not on Saturday. P conducts
workshop either just after or just before R. Q
conducts workshop in company C but before
Thursday. N conducts workshop before M. O
conducts workshop in company A. L does not
conduct workshop in company G and B.M does
not conduct workshop in company G.
 
In which company does M work?
(A) E
(B) A
(C) F
(D) B
(E) None of these
Q9
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions accordingly.
 
Five people John, Karan, Lalit, Mayank, and Nitin
go on holiday on five different days of the same
week starting from Monday. Karan goes on
holiday after Nitin. John goes on holiday
immediately before Mayank. At least one person
goes between Nitin and Karan. Two people go on
holiday between John and Lalit. Mayank goes on
holiday after Tuesday.
 
Find the correct combination.
(A) Lalit - Thursday
(B) John - Monday
(C) Karan - Thursday
(D) Mayank - Tuesday
(E) None is correct

(C) Three
(D) Two
(E) None of these

Q14
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.
M, C, S, A, E, K and H are seven classmates and
they have Birthdays on seven different days of
week (Starting from Sunday and ending on
Saturday) but not in the same order. M has a
Birthday on the day which starts from the same
letter as the day on which K has a Birthday starts
from the letter. K’s birthday does not start with S.
Only one person has a Birthday between K and
A. H has a Birthday immediately before S who
has her Birthday neither on Wednesday nor on
Friday. Less than two classmates have birthdays
between the birthday of H and the Birthday of E.
C has neither a Birthday immediately after the
Birthday of K nor has a Birthday on Friday.
How many days are there in between the
birthday of H and the birthday of C?
(A) One
(B) None
(C) Two
(D) Three
(E) More than three

Q18
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions accordingly.
 
Seven people go to work on 7 days of the week,
starting with Monday. Dev goes to work on one
of the days before Arav. Two people go to work
between Gianna and Boris. Cathy goes to work
before Gianna but after Eleanor. Only two people
go to work after Arav. Boris goes to work after
Gianna. Two people go to work between Dev and
Fiona. Fiona goes to work after Arav. Four people
go to work between Cathy and Boris.
 
Find the odd one out?
(A) Eleanor-Dev
(B) Arav-fiona
(C) Cathy-Gianna
(D) Boris-Arav
(E) Fiona-Gianna

Q23
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.
M, C, S, A, E, K and H are seven classmates and
they have Birthdays on seven different days of
week (Starting from Sunday and ending on
Saturday) but not in the same order. M has a
Birthday on the day which starts from the same
letter as the day on which K has a Birthday. K’s
birthday does not start with S. Only one person
has a Birthday between K and A. H has a Birthday
immediately before S who has her Birthday
neither on Wednesday nor on Friday. Less than
two classmates have birthdays between the
birthday of H and the Birthday of E. C has neither
a Birthday immediately after the Birthday of K
nor has a Birthday on Friday.
Which of the following statements is/are not
true?
(A) S has birthday on the last day of the week
(B) K has her birthday just after the day on which
E has birthday.
(C) A has birthday on Monday
(D) Both B and C
(E) Both A and C

Hints & Solutions
Q1
Text Solution:
Starting Point:
Clues:
1.) Two persons have meeting before the one,
who belongs to Egypt.
2.) E has meeting on Saturday.
3.) Person, who belongs to Mexico, has meeting
on Sunday.
4.) B has meeting two days before A, who
belongs to neither Egypt nor Mexico.
Inference:
From (4), B can have meeting on either Tuesday
or Wednesday.
Clues:
5.) F, who belongs to Italy, does not have meeting
on Monday.
Inference:
From (5), in case 2, F can have meeting on either
Thursday or Tuesday.
Clues:
6.) Person, who belongs to Germany, has meeting
just before C, who does not belong to Mexico.
7.) D belongs to Spain.
Inference:
From (6), case 2(b) must be rejected and from (7),
case 2(a) must be rejected. Also, G must belong
to Mexico.
F has meeting on Friday.
Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.
